name: Chicken Santa Fe
recipeIngredient: 2 Whole broiler-fryer chicken breasts, halved, skinned and boned
2 Sweet red peppers, roasted and skinned
recipeInstructions:
Marinade (Recipe below)
1. Place chicken between two pieces of wax paper.
2. Pound to 1/4 inch thickness using a meat mallet or similar flattening utensil on a hard surface.
3. In a large plastic zip-lock bag, place chicken in a single layer. Add marinade; seal the bag.
4. Refrigerate and marinate for 1 hour, turning once.
5. Bring chicken to room temperature; place on broiler pan and brush liberally with marinade.
6. Set oven temperature control at broil and arrange the rack so chicken is about 6 inches from heat.
7. Broil for 8 minutes, then turn the chicken and broil an additional 8 minutes, or until chicken is brown and a fork can be inserted with ease.
8. Brush chicken with melted jelly.
9. Place two roasted pepper strips to form an X on each breast half; spoon on remaining jelly.
10. Return to oven and broil until chicken is glazed.
Marinade:
1. In a medium bowl, mix together: 1/4 cup olive oil, juice and zest of 1 small lime, 1 clove garlic, crushed, 1 oz tequila, 1/4 teaspoon bottled hot pepper sauce, 1/8 teaspoon liquid smoke, and 1/4 teaspoon salt.
